zh.lifeSummary: 當Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussen於大約1470年出生時，他的父親Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussen31歲，母親Catharina josse van Veen21歲。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussen于大约1470年出生时，他的父亲Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussen31岁，母亲Catharina josse van Veen21岁。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Dussen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: patronymic from a nickname from Middle Dutch musch, modern Dutch mus ‘house sparrow’.
English: variant of   Musson  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Musson
meanings: English (East Midlands): nickname from Anglo-Norman French mussun, muisson ‘sparrow’.
English: variant of Muston with loss of -t-. The placename Muston (Leicestershire) is recorded as Musson several times in the 17th century.

French: habitational name from (Le) Musson, the name of a few places in various parts of France, e.g. in Meuse-et-Moselle, named with the Latin personal name Montius + the suffix -onem, or, alternatively, with Latin mons ‘mountain’ + the suffix -ionem.

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Arnoud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Arnold .

en.lifeSummary: When Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussen was born about 1470, his father, Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussen, was 31 and his mother, Catharina josse van Veen, was 21. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussen est né environ 1470, son père, Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussen, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Catharina josse van Veen, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussen um 1470 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussen 31 und seine Mutter Catharina josse van Veen 21. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussen è nato il circa 1470, il suo padre, Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussen, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Catharina josse van Veen, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussenが約1470年に生まれた時，父Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussenは31歳，母Catharina josse van Veenは21歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussen(이)가 대략 1470년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussen(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Catharina josse van Veen(은)는 21세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussen nasceu aproximadamente 1470, seu pai, Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussen, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Catharina josse van Veen, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussen родился около 1470 г., его отцу по имени Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussen было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Catharina josse van Veen, 21. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Peter Peter Arnoud van der Dussen nació aproximadamente 1470, su papá, Peter Arnoud Arnoud van der Dussen, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Catharina josse van Veen, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
